---
title: "Theorem 3.3 — Properties of the hat matrix"
lean_name: "HansenEconometrics.hatMatrix_idempotent"
chapter: 3
---

## Textbook statement

Hansen Theorem 3.3 establishes three properties of the **hat matrix**
(a.k.a. projection matrix)

$$
P = X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top:
$$

1. **Symmetric:** $P^\top = P$.
2. **Idempotent:** $P P = P$.
3. **Trace equals number of regressors:** $\mathrm{tr}(P) = k$.

Together these say $P$ is the orthogonal projection onto the column space
of $X$. The dual (annihilator) matrix $M = I - P$ is also symmetric,
idempotent, and has $\mathrm{tr}(M) = n-k$.

## Assumptions

- Design matrix $X \in \mathbb{R}^{n\times k}$.
- $X^\top X$ is invertible (full column rank).
- No distributional assumption — these are linear-algebraic facts about
  $P$.

## Lean statement

All three Lean theorems live in
[`HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean):

- [`hatMatrix`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L24)
  (definition, line 24) — `X * ⅟ (Xᵀ * X) * Xᵀ`.
- [`annihilatorMatrix`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L28)
  (definition, line 28) — `(1 : Matrix n n ℝ) - hatMatrix X`.
- [`hatMatrix_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L40)
  (theorem, line 40) — symmetry.
- [`hatMatrix_idempotent`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L104)
  (theorem, line 104) — idempotence.
- [`hatMatrix_trace`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L113)
  (theorem, line 113) — trace equals `Fintype.card k`.

Companion annihilator theorems:

- [`annihilatorMatrix_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L48)
  (line 48),
  [`annihilatorMatrix_idempotent`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L127)
  (line 127),
  [`annihilatorMatrix_trace`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L145)
  (line 145).

The three headline Lean theorems:

```lean
theorem hatMatrix_transpose
    (X : Matrix n k ℝ) [Invertible (Xᵀ * X)] :
    (hatMatrix X)ᵀ = hatMatrix X

theorem hatMatrix_idempotent
    (X : Matrix n k ℝ) [Invertible (Xᵀ * X)] :
    hatMatrix X * hatMatrix X = hatMatrix X

theorem hatMatrix_trace
    (X : Matrix n k ℝ) [Invertible (Xᵀ * X)] :
    Matrix.trace (hatMatrix X) = Fintype.card k
```

## Translation notes

- Hansen writes properties of $P$ informally; Lean splits them into one
  named theorem each.
- Trace in Lean returns the number of regressors as `Fintype.card k`, a
  `Nat`. Hansen calls it $k$. The
  [`annihilatorMatrix_trace`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L145)
  theorem casts to `ℝ` because it computes $n-k$ as a real (subtraction of
  `Nat`s would truncate at zero).
- Symmetry is stated as `(hatMatrix X)ᵀ = hatMatrix X`. There are also
  wrappers
  [`hatMatrix_isHermitian`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L157)
  (line 157) and
  [`annihilatorMatrix_isHermitian`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L151)
  (line 151) that package the same fact under Mathlib's `IsHermitian`
  predicate, used by spectral-theorem applications.
- Two related rank theorems are bundled in the same file:
  [`rank_hatMatrix`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L174)
  (line 174) gives $\mathrm{rank}(P) = k$, and
  [`rank_annihilatorMatrix_add`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L164)
  (line 164) gives $\mathrm{rank}(M) + k = n$. Both follow from the
  trace-equals-rank lemma for Hermitian idempotents, applied to $P$ or
  $M$.

## Proof sketch

**Symmetry.** Transpose the product:

$$
P^\top = (X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top)^\top = X ((X^\top X)^{-1})^\top X^\top.
$$

Since $X^\top X$ is symmetric, so is its inverse, and
$((X^\top X)^{-1})^\top = (X^\top X)^{-1}$. Hence $P^\top = P$.

**Idempotence.** Compute

$$
P P = X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top \cdot X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top
    = X(X^\top X)^{-1} (X^\top X) (X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top
    = X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top = P,
$$

where the middle product collapses via
$X^\top X \cdot (X^\top X)^{-1} = I$.

**Trace.** Use the cyclic invariance of trace:

$$
\mathrm{tr}(P) = \mathrm{tr}(X(X^\top X)^{-1} X^\top)
              = \mathrm{tr}((X^\top X)(X^\top X)^{-1})
              = \mathrm{tr}(I_k) = k.
$$

## Lean proof structure

- [`hatMatrix_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L40)
  (line 40): unfolds `hatMatrix`, distributes `Matrix.transpose_mul`,
  uses the helper
  [`inv_gram_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L33)
  (line 33) for `((Xᵀ * X)⁻¹)ᵀ = (Xᵀ * X)⁻¹`, which itself relies on
  [`gram_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L16)
  (line 16).
- [`hatMatrix_idempotent`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L104)
  (line 104): rewrites associatively then applies
  [`hat_mul_X`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L73)
  (line 73), the lemma $P X = X$ — itself an immediate consequence of
  $X^\top X \cdot (X^\top X)^{-1} = I$.
- [`hatMatrix_trace`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L113)
  (line 113): uses `Matrix.trace_mul_cycle` to rotate the product,
  collapses via `mul_invOf_self`, and finishes with `Matrix.trace_one`.

The annihilator companions reduce to the hat-matrix versions
algebraically:
[`annihilatorMatrix_idempotent`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L127)
expands $M = I - P$ and uses `hatMatrix_idempotent`;
[`annihilatorMatrix_trace`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L145)
uses linearity of trace;
[`annihilatorMatrix_transpose`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L48)
uses `hatMatrix_transpose`.

## Downstream uses

- [`rank_hatMatrix`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L174)
  (line 174) and
  [`rank_annihilatorMatrix_add`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L164)
  (line 164) — rank computations used in Chapter 5's distribution theory.
- [`fitted_eq_hat_mul_y`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L183)
  (line 183) — fitted vector identification.
- [`residual_eq_annihilator_mul_y`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3Projections.lean#L210)
  (line 210) — residual identification.
- The FWL theorems
  ([`Chapter3FWL.lean`](https://github.com/paulgp/lean-hansen-econometrics/blob/main/HansenEconometrics/Chapter3FWL.lean))
  consume `annihilator_mul_X`, `annihilatorMatrix_transpose`, etc.,
  heavily.
